title: allium ekimianum
intro: Allium ekimianum is a species of plant in the genus Allium, commonly known as the wild garlic or onion. It is native to the mountainous regions of southeastern Turkey and western Iran. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Allium ekimianum include reducing symptoms of colds and flu, and aiding in digestion, and herbal preparations often involve using the plant's leaves and bulbs in teas, tinctures, and infusions.
